content: Just as high school language arts educators teach explicit written reflection as a metacognitive skill to help students correct biased or incomplete reasoning, current LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be redesigned to borrow the structured, vocabulary-scaffolded reflection pedagogy from high English curricula to improve their reliability. This framework would train LLMs to explicitly label cognitive missteps in their outputs using structured reflection terminology, rather than relying on unstructured internal self-evaluation.
